text:
{
 "pov": "third person limited (Maren)",
 "tense": "present",
 "rhythm": "short declarative sentences broken by one longer swelling clause per paragraph; paragraph breaks fall like the turn of a tide",
 "banned": [
  "monster",
  "creature",
  "tentacle",
  "eldritch",
  "suddenly",
  "screamed"
 ],
 "notes": "The sleeper is never named or described as a body. It is known only by its effects: gutter of the lamp, pull of the tide, weight felt rather than seen. Keep Maren's interiority understated \u2014 she notes, she does not editorialize her own dread.",
 "diction": "spare, salt-scoured, working-class precision; concrete nouns over abstraction, dread carried by omission",
 "imagery": "images come from tide, weather, oil-light, and worked rope; the sea is never personified above a murmur \u2014 dread lives in objects and intervals, not similes",
 "dialogue": "speech is brief and unornamented, tags are said or nothing; silence and action carry what characters will not say"
}
